rationale: §11.251 governs school district planning and decision-making processes, committee composition, and administrative procedures. It contains no operative provisions regarding homestead exemptions, property tax revenue, or exemption amounts, so the proposed increase does not conflict.
quote: (d) The board shall also ensure that an administrative procedure is provided to clearly define the respective roles and responsibilities of the superintendent, central office staff, principals, teachers, district-level committee members, and campus-level committee members in the areas of planning, budgeting, curriculum, staffing patterns, staff development, and school organization.
_note: Educ. Code §11.251 addresses district- and campus-level planning and governance processes only. It references budgeting as an area of administrative responsibility but prescribes no tax policy, exemption amounts, or revenue calculations that would conflict with the proposed Tax Code §11.13(b) change.
